description_long: This house features a steeply pitched, multi-tiered wooden shingle roof with a prominent central gable and several intersecting rooflines. The exterior walls are clad in weathered wooden planks, with some sections showing whitewashed plaster, particularly on the left side where a chimney rises. Windows are small, rectangular, and arranged in symmetrical rows on the main facade, with a mix of single and grouped panes. The front includes a covered porch or balcony area with wooden railings and double wooden doors beneath. The structure has a rustic, traditional appearance, reminiscent of Eastern European or Alpine vernacular architecture. The overall form is tall and narrow, with a strong vertical emphasis and a robust, sturdy construction.
description: A tall, rustic wooden house with a steep, multi-tiered shingle roof, symmetrical windows, and a covered porch. The exterior combines weathered wood and whitewashed plaster, evoking a traditional, old-world style.
description_view_0: The front view shows the main entry with double wooden doors, a covered porch or balcony, and a prominent central gable roof.
description_view_1: This side view highlights the whitewashed plaster section, a visible chimney, and symmetrical window rows on the wooden facade.
description_view_2: The rear view displays a simpler wooden wall with fewer windows and the continuation of the steep, multi-tiered roof.
description_view_3: The opposite side view mirrors the first, showing the whitewashed section, chimney, and the arrangement of windows and rooflines.
attribute_entryway_design: Double wooden doors beneath a covered porch or balcony, with wooden railings and a simple overhang.
